source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: Microsoft's acquisition of Activision is a strategic metaverse/gaming platform play, and Microsoft has the most compelling AR/VR asset collection of any company
summary_extended: Baker argues that Microsoft's acquisition of Activision Blizzard, combined with its prior acquisitions of Bethesda and Minecraft, its Hololens military contract, and its dominance in Windows PC gaming, positions Microsoft to have the most pure AR/VR revenue of any publicly traded company. He views these moves as a deliberate, multi-year strategy to build a comprehensive gaming and metaverse platform rather than isolated M&A transactions. A key competitive implication is that Microsoft is slowly recapturing PC gaming market share from Steam (Valve), which remains private. Baker frames this as an analytical observation about Microsoft's strategic positioning in the metaverse/AR/VR space, noting the Activision deal specifically accelerates this platform buildout. The investment implication is that MSFT is the most compelling AR/VR equity available, with ATVI being a direct subject of the acquisition thesis itself.
detail: Microsoft's acquisitions of Bethesda, Activision, and Minecraft, combined with the Hololens military contract and Windows PC gaming dominance, give it the most pure AR/VR revenue of any company. Baker views this as Microsoft building a comprehensive gaming/metaverse platform and slowly recapturing PC gaming from Steam.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as an analytical observation about Microsoft's strategic positioning rather than an explicit investment recommendation.
quote_fragment: almost certainly going to have the most pure AR VR revenue of any company

ticker_audit.reasoning: Microsoft is the central subject of the thesis — Baker is specifically analyzing its strategic AR/VR and gaming platform positioning built through a series of acquisitions and organic assets.
Activision Blizzard is the target of the Microsoft acquisition that anchors this thesis, making it a direct subject of the M&A and metaverse platform argument.
